perturbation.original_text: At any time, upon the respective Disclosing Party's request, Recipient shall promptly deliver to the respective Disclosing Party the Confidential Informa tion and all materials relating to or involving Confidential Information (whether prepared by th e respective Disclosing Party or otherwise), and will not retain any copies, extracts or other repr oductions in whole or in part regarding the Confidential Information. The delivery of such mate rial shall not relieve Recipient of its obligation of confidentiality or other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon reasonable notice from the Disclosing Party, Recipient shall endeavor to return the Confidential Information. Recipient may retain copies of materials to comply with internal document retention policies.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'At any time, upon the respective Disclosing Party's request, Recipient shall promptly deliver' to 'Upon reasonable notice from the Disclosing Party, Recipient shall endeavor to return' weakens the obligation and introduces ambiguity regarding the timing and completeness of the return. Allowing the Recipient to 'retain copies of materials to comply with internal document retention policies' directly contradicts the original intent to prevent any reproductions of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon reasonable notice from the Disclosing Party, Recipient shall endeavor to return the Confidential Information. Recipient may retain copies of materials to comply with internal document retention policies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the obligation to return confidential information and introduces ambiguity. Specifically, changing 'shall promptly deliver' to 'shall endeavor to return' introduces uncertainty about the timing and completeness. Moreover, allowing the Recipient to 'retain copies of materials' directly contradicts the original intent to prevent any reproductions of the Confidential Information. Therefore, the changed text creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, as the contradicted text restates the Changed Text word for word. It is a strong contradiction

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall be governed by the laws of Belgium. All disputes or differences arising in connection with this agreement which cannot be settled amicably shall be finally settled by arbitration in Brussels under the Rules of Arbitra tion of the International Chamber of Commerce by one or more arbitrators to be appointed under the te rms of those rules. In any arbitration in which there are three arbitrators, the chairman shall be of juridical education. It shall be conducted in English. The award of the arbitration will be final and binding upon the parties concerned. The parties concerned may instead elect to resolve by mediation a dispute or difference arising in connection with this agreement which cannot be settled amicably.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall be governed by the laws of Belgium. All disputes or differences arising in connection with this agreement shall be subject to non-binding mediation before any legal proceedings may be initiated.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies binding arbitration in Brussels, whereas the modified clause specifies non-binding mediation. This introduces conflicting dispute resolution mechanisms. The original text has a pre-defined arbitration agreement, while the new version requires a non-binding mediation before any legal actions, creating ambiguity about the binding arbitration method, and essentially, removing it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement shall be governed by the laws of Belgium. All disputes or differences arising in connection with this agreement shall be subject to non-binding mediation before any legal proceedings may be initiated.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates binding arbitration, while the changed text mandates non-binding mediation before any legal proceedings. This directly contradicts the original provision for binding arbitration, essentially removing it as an option and creating a conflict in dispute resolution mechanisms. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, emphasizes this shift to non-binding mediation only, further highlighting the incompatibility with the initial binding arbitration agreement. This fits the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition because it creates conflicting obligations in dispute resolution.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations pursuant to Article 2 shall remain valid for the Recipient even after the end of this agreemen t for a period of five (5) years thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations pursuant to Article 2 shall remain valid for the Recipient for one (1) year after the end of this agreement, unless otherwise agreed in writing.
perturbation.explanation: Shortening the duration of the confidentiality obligation from five years to one year directly contradicts the original intention and provides an easy out with written consent. The five-year clause has a set timeframe whereas the new duration is subjected to a different change in the clause, causing in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement shall come into force after signatu re of both parties hereto on the Effective Date and shall expire on February 28, 2009. The obligations pursuant to Article 2 shall remain valid for the Recipient for one (1) year after the end of this agreement, unless otherwise agreed in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a five-year obligation, while the changed text reduces it to one year with an 'unless otherwise agreed' clause. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which contains the one-year obligation with the 'unless otherwise agreed' clause, as it replaces the original five-year commitment. This creates a clear contradiction because the parties are now bound by the one-year term, subject to written agreement, instead of the original five-year term as initially intended, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes.
